title: defect_root_cause_analysis.py output
description: Always a flat object — defect_root_cause_analysis.py requires --component. Only rows where has_md_event is True are included in all four arrays. supplier_criticality_breakdown is supplier_schedule_impact split further by component_criticality — the only one of this operation's three original axes (fault type / supplier / project) that gains the criticality-split second level; fault_type_breakdown and project_schedule_impact stay single-level. Named supplier_schedule_impact / supplier_criticality_breakdown rather than the generic component_breakdown / criticality_breakdown pair used elsewhere, since this op already has three pre-existing breakdown axes and the generic names would read ambiguously next to fault_type_breakdown / project_schedule_impact.

fault_type_breakdown.description: Pareto of defect events by md_fault_type.

md_fault_type.description: "Unspecified" when md_fault_type is blank on a defect row.

pct_of_defects.description: 0-100. This fault type's share of all defect events in scope.

supplier_schedule_impact.description: Per-supplier defect frequency, fault attribution, and total schedule days lost.

pct_of_component_days_lost.description: 0-100. This supplier's share of total days lost across all suppliers in scope.

supplier_criticality_breakdown.description: Same fields as supplier_schedule_impact, grouped by (supplier_name, component_criticality) instead of supplier_name alone. pct_of_component_days_lost is recomputed relative to this array's own total_days_lost sum, not supplier_schedule_impact's.

pct_of_component_days_lost.description: 0-100. This row's share of total days lost across all rows in this array.

project_schedule_impact.description: Per-project defect frequency and total schedule days lost. Empty array when project_number isn't populated for any defect row.
